meta.description: Flattened research node graph for the .knowledge/ Instructional Knowledge Engine. Optimized for RAG retrieval and agent navigation. Every node includes a terminal_objective block.

nodes.title: Project-Based Learning (PBL)
nodes.description: Instructional methodology where students acquire knowledge through real-world projects. Covers the Gold Standard PBL framework, driving questions, authentic products, PBL in IT education, failure modes, and implementation checklist.

nodes.title: Instructional Scaffolding
nodes.description: Temporary instructional support within the Zone of Proximal Development (ZPD). Covers content scaffolds, process scaffolds, code scaffolds (starter code, faded worked examples), social scaffolds, metacognitive scaffolds, and fading strategies.

nodes.title: Cognitive Load Theory (CLT)
nodes.description: Framework for human learning based on working memory limitations. Covers intrinsic, extraneous, and germane cognitive load; CLT effects (worked example, split-attention, redundancy, expertise reversal, modality); and instructional design checklist.

nodes.title: Bloom's Taxonomy (Revised)
nodes.description: Hierarchical framework classifying educational objectives into six cognitive levels: Remember, Understand, Apply, Analyze, Evaluate, Create. Covers learning objective writing, action verbs, assessment alignment, and IT curriculum sequencing.

nodes.title: IT Education Strategies
nodes.description: Synthesis of strategies specific to IT education: concept-first sequencing, competency-based progression, authentic assessment, the novice-to-practitioner transition, spiral curriculum, threshold concepts, and research-backed practice table.

nodes.title: Programming Language Fundamentals
nodes.description: Universal programming concepts: values and types, static vs. dynamic typing, variables, operators, control flow, functions, scope, closures, memory management, and error handling. Language-agnostic; examples in JavaScript and Python.

nodes.title: Programming Paradigms
nodes.description: Major programming paradigms: imperative, object-oriented (OOP), functional (FP), declarative, and event-driven. Multi-paradigm language table, paradigm selection guide, and web development paradigm mapping.

nodes.title: Software Engineering Principles
nodes.description: Durable design principles: DRY, YAGNI, KISS, Separation of Concerns, SOLID (SRP, OCP, LSP, ISP, DIP), Gang of Four design patterns (creational, structural, behavioral), and Clean Code heuristics.

nodes.title: Programming Pedagogy
nodes.description: Research-backed strategies for teaching programming: the notional machine, PRIMM methodology, Parsons Problems, Peer Instruction, live coding, language selection criteria, common anti-patterns, and assessment strategies.

nodes.title: Core Competencies for Full-Stack Web Development
nodes.description: Tiered competency map for full-stack web development: Tier 0 (foundations), Tier 1 (frontend core), Tier 2 (frontend advanced), Tier 3 (backend core), Tier 4 (backend advanced), Tier 5 (DevOps/infrastructure), Tier 6 (professional skills). Industry alignment notes for 2025.

nodes.title: Frontend Technology Stack
nodes.description: HTML5 (semantic elements, accessibility, content model), CSS3 (box model, flexbox, grid, custom properties, Tailwind), JavaScript/TypeScript (ES6+, DOM, Event Loop, async), React (components, hooks, ecosystem), and Vite build tooling.

nodes.title: Backend Technology Stack
nodes.description: Node.js (event loop, modules, npm, environment variables), Express.js (routing, middleware, error handling), REST API design (resource naming, HTTP methods, status codes), PostgreSQL (SQL, relationships, indexes), Prisma ORM, authentication (bcrypt, JWT, OAuth), and input validation/security.

nodes.title: Modern Web Standards
nodes.description: Standards bodies (W3C, WHATWG, IETF, ECMA), HTML Living Standard, CSS modules and browser compatibility, ECMAScript version history (ES6+), HTTP/1.1/2/3, HTTPS/TLS, WCAG 2.1 (POUR principles, conformance levels), and browser Web Platform APIs.

nodes.title: Web Infrastructure and Deployment
nodes.description: Static vs. dynamic deployment types, Netlify/Vercel/GitHub Pages (static), Render/Railway/Fly.io (backend), DNS record types and resolution, HTTPS/TLS and security headers, environment management (.env, Twelve-Factor App), Docker (images, containers, Compose), and CI/CD with GitHub Actions.

nodes.title: Master Research Index
nodes.description: Complete listing of all 16 research nodes organized by domain with descriptions, file paths, and key questions answered. Entry point for human navigation of the .knowledge/ knowledge base.

nodes.title: Cross-Reference Map
nodes.description: Directed relationship graph showing all inbound and outbound links between research nodes. Includes relationship density summary table identifying the most central nodes in the graph.

nodes.title: Git Foundations
nodes.description: Foundational knowledge of Git: VCS history (local→centralized→distributed), Git's snapshot-based DAG data model (blobs, trees, commits), the three working areas (working directory, staging, repository), core commands (init, clone, add, commit, status, log, diff, branch, switch, merge, push, pull, fetch), merge conflict resolution, and GitHub remote workflow.

nodes.title: Git Best Practices
nodes.description: Atomic commits, Conventional Commits specification, commit message quality, branching strategies (Git Flow / GitHub Flow / trunk-based), staging with git add -p, interactive rebase, annotated tags, SemVer, .gitignore hygiene, pre-commit hooks, PR workflow, and common mistakes.

nodes.title: 2026 Tech Trend Gap Analysis
nodes.description: Validates internal research coverage against 2026 high-growth industry skill demands. Classifies 15 gaps as CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW. Priority action plan for new research document creation. CRITICAL gaps: Automated Testing, LLM API Integration, MCP, Agent Orchestration.
